lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The chapel is the only room in St. Brendan's that does not announce itself with a function. No board. No bay numbers. No overhead fluorescents humming their particular frequency of institutional indifference. Two rows of pew. An unlit candle on the sill. A woman already seated.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I didn't know you'd be here.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: I'm always here at this hour.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I should go.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: You should sit.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: A beat. He sits one row behind her — not quite facing her, not quite avoiding her either. The complaint is in his locker. He has known this for six hours.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: There's a form.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: I know about the form.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I haven't filed it.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: I know that too.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She turns — not fully, just enough to let him know the angle has changed.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: The diversion in February. Ward 4-North. The man who came in cardiac, routed through the 3-West corridor because Harte had closed Bay Seven.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: You knew.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: His wife sat with me for four hours afterward. I know what a death looks like when the room it happened in wasn't the right room.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo rests his forearms on the pew in front of him. His hands are open — a gesture that belongs to neither prayer nor accusation. Somewhere above them the building breathes: the ventilation system, the elevator shaft, the weight of a hospital at three in the morning.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: The form names that death.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: It should.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: If I file it, Harte already knows it's coming. The performance review landed this afternoon.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: I heard.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Then you understand why I'm sitting here instead of at the administrator's desk.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: I understand why you think you are.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: A long silence. The candle on the sill is not lit. It holds its shape. That is the only thing unlit candles can offer.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: There's a second name.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Upstairs. Right now. 4-North again — same corridor, same routing logic, because the policy hasn't changed. A woman named Renata Sobol. Sixty-three. Came in four hours ago with a rhythm Maeve flagged as high priority. Waiting on a monitored bed that Harte's occupancy numbers say doesn't exist.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo does not stand. He does not move at all. This is different from stillness. This is something locked.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: How do you know that name?

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: The same way I know every name. People come here when the machinery stops working and they've run out of corridors. A nurse sat in that pew at eleven o'clock. Couldn't tell me what to do. Could only tell me what was happening.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Renata Sobol.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: She has a daughter. The daughter is in the waiting room now, doing what families do when no one tells them enough.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The building hums around them. Theo looks at the unlit candle for a long time.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: If I file tonight—

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Something happens. Yes.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Or nothing happens. They receive it. They log it. They refer it. Harte's lawyers have seen fifty of these.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Perhaps.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: The first man died. The form has his name. That wasn't enough to make me sign it.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: And now there's a second name.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: She might be fine.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: She might.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Neither of them fills the silence that follows, which is not the same as leaving it empty. Agnes turns back toward the front of the chapel. Her hands are folded — not in prayer, more the way a person holds something carried too long.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: I'm not going to tell you what the form means. You already know what it means.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: It means I've documented two patients harmed by a policy I watched being written and did nothing.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: You've been doing something. Slowly. In the way people do things when they're afraid.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: That's not a defence.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: No. It isn't.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He stands. His uniform carries the particular creases of a long shift. He looks at the door — not the one he came through, but the one at the back, the staff stairwell, the one that goes up.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: 4-North.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Room 408. Last I heard.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: If I go up there—

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: You'll know her face. And then you'll know what you already know, with one more thing added.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He doesn't answer. He straightens — a small adjustment that is not quite resolution and not quite its absence. He moves toward the back door. At the threshold he stops.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: The form has a date line. I haven't put anything on it.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: There's still tonight.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He pushes through the door. The chapel stays as it was — the unlit candle, two rows of pew, a woman who holds names the way the building holds its wiring: everywhere, unseen, necessary. Above her, on the fourth floor, a bed is occupied by someone whose name she has already learned to carry.
